transcript.text: I keep sketching the same building in my head, but every time I try to draft something new I feel like I'm failing. The breakup left me feeling empty and my work feels like a dead end. I keep putting off the first draft because I can't shake the thought that I'm not good enough.

You're really struggling to get started on new projects because you're worried you're not good enough, and that's a tough spot to be in. It sounds like the breakup has left you feeling a bit lost, and your work isn't giving you the fulfillment you're looking for. Can you tell me more about what's holding you back from drafting something new?

Every time I try to draft, I feel like I'm setting myself up for failure. The breakup just left me questioning who I am, and I keep putting it off because I'm terrified of being judged. I also worry about my health, but that just adds to the paralysis.

You're feeling really stuck right now because the thought of being judged is terrifying, and it's holding you back from even starting new projects. It's like the fear of failure is looming over you, making it hard to take that first step. The breakup has already left you feeling uncertain about who you are, and now this fear is adding another layer of complexity. Yeah, it's a lot to navigate, but let's try to break it down - what do you think would happen if you did start drafting something new, and it wasn't perfect?

If I start drafting something imperfect, I might just see it as a rough sketch, but I still fear the structure will collapse under scrutiny. That thought makes me hesitate to even lay the first beam. It feels like the breakup and my health worries are all piled on top of the same unfinished façade.

You're hesitant to start drafting because you're afraid that your work won't be perfect, and that fear is holding you back. It's like you're putting a lot of pressure on yourself to create something that will withstand scrutiny, and that's a heavy burden to carry. It makes sense that you'd feel that way, especially after a breakup that's left you feeling uncertain about who you are. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're starting from scratch, but maybe we can explore what it would mean to create something that's not perfect, but still meaningful to you.

I keep sketching the same façade in my mind, but every draft feels like a collapse. The breakup still feels like a cracked foundation and I'm terrified of adding another beam that might not hold. My body keeps telling me I'm not ready, and I end up putting the plan off until the next week.

You're still carrying the weight of that breakup, and it's like the fear of judgment is looming over you, making it hard to take that first step. It sounds like your mind is racing with thoughts of collapse, and your body is telling you to slow down. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're starting from scratch, but what if you allowed yourself to create something imperfect, just to see what happens? Maybe we can explore what's holding you back from taking that first step, and what it would mean to create something that's not perfect, but still meaningful to you.

I guess I could try a rough sketch, but the thought of it looking like a failure after the breakup feels like admitting I'm not good enough. That idea makes me pause, and I end up putting it off again.

You're really struggling with the idea that trying a rough sketch feels like admitting you're not good enough, and that's making you pause. It's like the fear of judgment is still lingering from the breakup, and it's affecting your ability to start new projects. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're starting from scratch, but what if you allowed yourself to create something imperfect, just to see what happens? Maybe it's not about the end result, but about taking that first step, and seeing where it takes you.

I keep drafting over and over, but each time I imagine the critique and my own doubts as a structural failure, like a building collapsing before the foundation even sets. The breakup still feels like a cracked cornerstone, and I hesitate to even lay a single beam because I think the end result will confirm I'm not good enough. Maybe I should test the idea with a simple sketch, but the fear of judgment keeps me procrastinating.

You're still feeling really uncertain about your abilities, and the fear of judgment is holding you back from even starting a new project. It's like the breakup has left you with a cracked foundation, and you're hesitant to add any new beams to it. I can see why you'd feel that way, especially when you're imagining the critique and your own doubts as a structural failure. Maybe we can explore what it would mean to create something that's not perfect, but still meaningful to you. What if you allowed yourself to create a simple sketch, just to see what happens, and we can work from there to build your confidence and self-esteem?

Maybe I could just lay down a rough outline, but the image of a draft that looks like a failed structure keeps me from even touching the paper. I keep thinking that if the first line is wrong, it's as if the whole building collapses, and that anxiety about my own health only amplifies that dread. It feels like I'm stuck on a scaffolding that I can't lower, and I don't know how to get the first beam in place.

You're really struggling to start drafting because the fear of judgment is overwhelming, and it's like the whole project will collapse if the first line is wrong. It makes sense that you'd feel that way, especially after a breakup that's left you questioning who you are. The image of a failed structure is a powerful one, and it's holding you back from even touching the paper. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're starting from scratch, but what if you allowed yourself to create something imperfect, just to see what happens? Maybe we can explore what it would mean to create something that's not perfect, but still meaningful to you.

I keep thinking the first line will be a disaster, and that feels like admitting I'm not good enough. The breakup still feels like a cracked foundation, and I keep putting the plan off. Maybe a rough sketch won't help if I can't stop the internal critique.

The breakup still feels like a cracked foundation, and it's like that's affecting every new project you try to start. You're hesitant to even lay down a rough outline because the fear of judgment is overwhelming, and it's like the whole project will collapse if the first line is wrong. What's the smallest step you could take to start drafting, even if it's just a simple sketch? Maybe we can explore what it would mean to create something imperfect, just to see what happens, and work from there to build your confidence and self-esteem.

I could try a one‑line sketch, but the thought that it won’t meet my own standards feels like admitting defeat. The breakup still feels like a cracked foundation, and I keep putting it off because I worry about my health and the judgment of others. Even a small draft feels like a structural failure in my mind.

The thought of not meeting your own standards is really daunting, and it's like the fear of judgment is still looming over you. I could start with a one‑line sketch, but thinking it won’t meet my standards feels like admitting I’m not good enough. The breakup still feels like a cracked foundation, so I keep pushing it off, worrying about my health and what others will say. Even a tiny draft feels like a structural failure in my mind.

You're hesitant to even start a one-line sketch because you're worried it won't meet your own standards, and that feels like admitting defeat. It's like the fear of judgment is still looming over you, and it's connected to the breakup, which still feels like a cracked foundation.
